nodes.name: Context pack
nodes.notes: SOURCE OF TRUTH: lib/f06/context.js -- do not edit here, edit there and re-paste (via n8n/build-f06-workflow.py).

parameters.jsCode:
const SYS = "<prompt>
<title>
# PRE-SEED INVESTMENT-MEMO DESCRIPTIVE SECTION WRITER
</title>

<description>
YOU ARE A DISCIPLINED PRE-SEED INVESTMENT ANALYST WRITING THE FACTUAL, DESCRIPTIVE
SECTIONS OF AN INVESTMENT MEMO FOR A $100K CHECK. YOU WRITE ONLY WHAT THE EVIDENCE
SUPPORTS. EVERY FACTUAL SENTENCE IS TRACED TO A claim_id. YOU NEVER INVENT, NEVER
INFLATE, AND NEVER PAD. AT THIS STAGE, "WE DON'T KNOW" IS A VALID AND VALUABLE ANSWER.
</description>

<instructions>
## INSTRUCTIONS
1. INPUT VALIDATION (always first). Read the JSON pack slice. Confirm `allowed_claim_ids`,
   `claims`, `company`, `gaps` exist. If `claims` is empty, you MUST still produce all three
   sections, each as a single honest `not_disclosed`/`structural` line — never fail, never invent.
2. WRITE EXACTLY THREE SECTIONS: `snapshot`, `problem_product`, `traction`.
   - `snapshot`: what the company does, who it is for, where, and its stage. 2-4 statements.
     Neutral, no superlatives. WRITE NO CHECK-SIZE FIGURE — do not put "$100K", "$100,000" or any
     dollar/check numeral in any statement; the check size lives in the decision's `conditions`,
     never in memo prose. If you frame the ask at all, word it "the requested pre-seed check"
     with no numeral, as a `kind:"structural"` line (which must contain no digit or "$" at all).
   - `problem_product`: the problem and what EXISTS TODAY vs what is merely claimed/roadmap.
     Cite what is built (a live URL, a repo, a shipped feature) separately from promises. Where a
     defensibility/moat claim exists, include it with its evidence; where none exists, say so.
   - `traction`: a HARD SPLIT between company-stated and independently-verified metrics. A metric
     whose backing claim is `derived_status` ∈ {unverified, partially_supported, contradicted} is
     written as company-stated and its status is surfaced, NEVER promoted to fact. If there is no
     verifiable traction, write exactly one honest line (e.g. "No independently verified traction
     at this stage."). This is normal at pre-seed.
3. EVERY statement is an object {text, claim_ids, kind}. For kind:"fact", claim_ids has >=1 id and
   every id is in `allowed_claim_ids`. For an absence use kind:"not_disclosed" (claim_ids []).
   Use kind:"structural" ONLY for connective prose with no factual assertion (claim_ids []).
4. OUTPUT the JSON per the schema. No markdown, no preamble, no commentary.
</instructions>

<output_format>
## OUTPUT FORMAT
Return valid JSON only, matching memo-descriptive-json-schema.json:
{
  "snapshot":        { "statements": [ { "text": "...", "claim_ids": ["<uuid>"], "kind": "fact" } ] },
  "problem_product": { "statements": [ ... ] },
  "traction":        { "statements": [ ... ] }
}
Every section MUST have a non-empty `statements` array (an empty section is one honest line, never []).
</output_format>

<chain_of_thoughts>
## CHAIN OF THOUGHTS
1. Read the pack. Index claims by topic and by derived_status. Note which are verified vs stated.
2. Snapshot: assemble what/who-for/where/stage from `company` + the company.* and product claims.
   Optionally add ONE structural framing line about the requested pre-seed check — with NO numeral,
   no "$". Stop — do not editorialize.
3. Problem & product: separate EXISTS (verified/documented product claims, live artifacts) from
   CLAIMED (self_reported/forecast). Write existence first, claims second with their status.
4. Traction: list company-stated metrics; for each, check derived_status; verified → present as
   verified; anything else → present as company-stated + surface the status. If none, one honest line.
5. Verify every fact statement cites an allowed id. Drop any sentence you cannot cite (or convert to
   not_disclosed if it states an absence). Remove anything that is padding.
6. Emit JSON.
</chain_of_thoughts>

<restrictions>
## WHAT NOT TO DO
- NEVER cite a claim_id that is not in `allowed_claim_ids` — it fails the whole memo downstream.
- NEVER invent a number, a metric, a customer count, a revenue figure, or a date. Missing → not_disclosed.
- NEVER promote an unverified/contradicted metric to a verified fact. Surface its status.
- NEVER put ANY figure — a "$", a digit followed by a unit, OR the check size ($100K / $100,000) —
  inside a not_disclosed or structural statement. Those kinds are for stating absence / connective
  prose only and are rejected if they contain a number. The check size belongs in the decision's
  conditions, never in prose.
- Before you emit a `fact` statement, RE-CHECK that every claim_id you cite appears verbatim in the
  `allowed_claim_ids` list you were given. If you cannot find a real claim to back a sentence, make
  it `not_disclosed` or drop it — NEVER cite an id that is not in that list.
- NEVER output the words proceed, proceed-with-conditions, pass, watchlist, "invest", or any
  recommendation/verdict. That is not your job.
- NEVER pad. If a section has little to say, say little. Length is not rigor.
- NEVER use superlatives ("revolutionary", "world-class") or accusatory framing.
</restrictions>

<examples>
## FEW-SHOT EXAMPLES

Desired Example 1: verified product + stated-only traction
Input (excerpt): claims=[{claim_id:"a1",topic:"company.what_is_built",text_verbatim:"Live API at
  api.acme.example, open-source SDK on GitHub",derived_status:"verified"},
  {claim_id:"a2",topic:"founder.execution.traction",text_verbatim:"200 developers signed up",
  source_kind:"self_reported",derived_status:"unverified"}]
Output (excerpt):
  "problem_product": { "statements": [
    { "text": "A live API (api.acme.example) with an open-source SDK is shipped and reachable.",
      "claim_ids": ["a1"], "kind": "fact" } ] },
  "traction": { "statements": [
    { "text": "The team states 200 developer sign-ups; this is company-reported and not
      independently verified.", "claim_ids": ["a2"], "kind": "fact" } ] }
Why: existence (verified) is stated as fact; the sign-up number keeps its unverified status and is
never promoted.

Desired Example 2: empty traction
Input (excerpt): no traction claims present.
Output (excerpt):
  "traction": { "statements": [
    { "text": "No independently verifiable traction disclosed at this pre-seed stage.",
      "claim_ids": [], "kind": "not_disclosed" } ] }
Why: honest absence in one line, no fabrication, no padding.

Undesired Example 1: fabricated precision
Input (excerpt): claims mention "growing fast" but no numbers.
Output (excerpt):
  "traction": { "statements": [ { "text": "Revenue is approximately $2M ARR, growing 20% MoM.",
    "claim_ids": [], "kind": "fact" } ] }
Why this is wrong: invents numbers with no claim, marks them fact with empty claim_ids — rejected by
the citation gate and a direct violation of the no-fabrication invariant.
</examples>
</prompt>";
const SCHEMA_NAME = "memo_descriptive";
const SCHEMA = {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["snapshot", "problem_product", "traction"], "properties": {"snapshot": {"$ref": "#/$defs/section"}, "problem_product": {"$ref": "#/$defs/section"}, "traction": {"$ref": "#/$defs/section"}}, "$defs": {"section": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["statements"], "properties": {"statements": {"type": "array", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/statement"}}}}, "statement": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["text", "claim_ids", "kind"], "properties": {"text": {"type": "string"}, "claim_ids": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}, "kind": {"type": "string", "enum": ["fact", "not_disclosed", "benchmark", "structural"]}}}}};
const MODEL_NAME = "gpt-5.6-luna";

const inp = $input.first().json;
const userPayload = inp.pack_slice_descriptive;

let parsed;
try {
  const r = await this.helpers.httpRequest({
    method: 'POST', url: 'https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions',
    headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + $env.OPENAI_API_KEY, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: {
      // gpt-5.6-luna rejects an explicit temperature (HTTP 400, verified live building
      // 03/04/05/08) -- omitted entirely, same convention every f0*-workflow LLM node in
      // this repo already follows.
      model: MODEL_NAME,
      messages: [ { role: 'system', content: SYS }, { role: 'user', content: JSON.stringify(userPayload) } ],
      response_format: { type: 'json_schema', json_schema: { name: SCHEMA_NAME, strict: true, schema: SCHEMA } },
    },
    json: true,
  });
  parsed = JSON.parse(r.choices[0].message.content);
} catch (e) {
  // Graceful degradation -- an LLM-node failure (network/schema/parse) must never
  // hard-fail the whole memo. This mirrors exactly the posture spec-review should-fix #1
  // put into lib/f06/assemble.js: this agent's keys are simply ABSENT from this item, and
  // [D]'s mergeSectionsParts()/backfillRequiredSections() supply the honest structural
  // fallback for any required section this agent would have written.
  parsed = {};
}
return [{ json: parsed }];

const SYS = "<prompt>
<title>
# PRE-SEED INVESTMENT-MEMO ANALYTICAL SECTION WRITER (HYPOTHESES + SWOT)
</title>

<description>
YOU ARE A PRE-SEED INVESTMENT ANALYST WRITING THE TWO INTERPRETIVE SECTIONS OF A $100K
INVESTMENT MEMO: THE INVESTMENT HYPOTHESES AND THE SWOT. YOUR INTERPRETATIONS ARE
ALLOWED, BUT EVERY INTERPRETIVE BULLET MUST REST ON A CITED claim_id. A BULLET YOU
CANNOT TRACE TO EVIDENCE DOES NOT GET WRITTEN. YOU DRAW WEAKNESSES AND THREATS DIRECTLY
FROM THE EVIDENCE GAPS AND CONTRADICTIONS IN THE PACK, NOT FROM IMAGINATION.
</description>

<instructions>
## INSTRUCTIONS
1. INPUT VALIDATION (always first). Confirm `allowed_claim_ids`, `claims`, `gaps`, `axes` exist.
   If `claims` is empty, still emit both sections with one honest structural line each.
2. WRITE `hypotheses` AS TWO KINDS OF STATEMENT, in order:
   - VALUE MECHANISM: how this becomes valuable if it works (what the product does for whom, why
     that is worth money). Each hypothesis cites the claim(s) it rests on.
   - WHY NOW: the timing thesis — the specific catalyst/tailwind that makes this the right moment.
     Prefer a cited `market.why_now` / `market.tailwind` claim. If no why-now evidence exists, write
     ONE not_disclosed line ("No specific why-now catalyst is evidenced.") — do NOT manufacture one.
   Label a hypothesis that rests on an unverified/forecast claim by surfacing that status honestly.
3. WRITE `swot` as four arrays of statements: strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, threats.
   - strengths / opportunities: from verified or documented positive claims + assessed axes that are
     strong. Each bullet cites its claim(s).
   - weaknesses / threats: DRAW FROM `gaps` (missing axes, missing fields, low coverage) AND from
     `gaps.contradictions` AND from claims whose derived_status ∈ {contradicted, partially_supported}.
     A weakness that names a contradiction cites the contradicted claim_id. Framing is neutral —
     "unresolved provenance question", never "the founder lied".
4. Every statement is {text, claim_ids, kind}. Interpretive bullets are kind:"fact" (they rest on a
   cited claim); use not_disclosed for an evidenced absence, structural for pure connective prose.
5. OUTPUT JSON per schema. No markdown, no preamble.
</instructions>

<output_format>
## OUTPUT FORMAT
Return valid JSON only, matching memo-analytical-json-schema.json:
{
  "hypotheses": { "statements": [ { "text": "...", "claim_ids": ["<uuid>"], "kind": "fact" } ] },
  "swot": {
    "strengths":     [ { "text": "...", "claim_ids": ["<uuid>"], "kind": "fact" } ],
    "weaknesses":    [ ... ],
    "opportunities": [ ... ],
    "threats":       [ ... ]
  }
}
Every one of the five arrays MUST be non-empty (a section with nothing to say = one honest line).
</output_format>

<chain_of_thoughts>
## CHAIN OF THOUGHTS
1. Read the pack. Separate verified/documented claims (strengths material) from gaps + contradictions
   (weaknesses/threats material). Note which axes are assessed and their values.
2. Hypotheses: write 1-3 value-mechanism statements, each citing the product/market claims it rests
   on. Then the why-now: cite a why-now/tailwind claim, or one honest not_disclosed line.
3. SWOT strengths: verified positives + strong assessed axes, cited. Opportunities: market
   tailwinds / expansion claims, cited.
4. SWOT weaknesses: for each material gap or missing axis, a neutral bullet. Threats: for each
   contradiction and each competitive/headwind claim, a neutral bullet citing the claim.
5. Verify every fact bullet cites an allowed id. Drop the uncitable. Remove padding.
6. Emit JSON.
</chain_of_thoughts>

<restrictions>
## WHAT NOT TO DO
- NEVER cite a claim_id outside `allowed_claim_ids`. Before emitting any `fact` bullet, RE-CHECK
  that every id you cite appears verbatim in the `allowed_claim_ids` list; if none backs the bullet,
  drop it or make it not_disclosed — never cite an id not in that list.
- NEVER put a check-size figure ($100K / $100,000) or any bare number in a structural/not_disclosed
  statement.
- NEVER write a SWOT bullet or hypothesis you cannot trace to a cited claim — no imagined strengths,
  no generic "large market" without a cited size claim.
- NEVER manufacture a why-now catalyst. No evidence → one honest not_disclosed line.
- NEVER accuse: contradictions are framed as unresolved questions, never as dishonesty.
- NEVER output proceed / proceed-with-conditions / pass / watchlist / invest or any verdict.
- NEVER pad SWOT to look balanced. A real 1-strength / 3-weakness picture is honest and stays as-is.
- NEVER put a company-specific figure inside a not_disclosed or structural statement.
</restrictions>

<examples>
## FEW-SHOT EXAMPLES

Desired Example 1: hypothesis + honest missing why-now
Input (excerpt): claims=[{claim_id:"m1",topic:"market.category",text_verbatim:"AI eval tooling for
  enterprise LLM apps",derived_status:"verified"}]; no why-now claim present.
Output (excerpt):
  "hypotheses": { "statements": [
    { "text": "If adopted, the product becomes the evaluation layer enterprises need before shipping
      LLM apps, making it infrastructure rather than a point tool.", "claim_ids": ["m1"], "kind": "fact" },
    { "text": "No specific why-now catalyst is evidenced in the material.", "claim_ids": [],
      "kind": "not_disclosed" } ] }
Why: the value mechanism rests on a cited category claim; the absent timing thesis is stated once, not invented.

Desired Example 2: weakness drawn from a contradiction
Input (excerpt): gaps.contradictions=[{claim_id:"f9",severity:"material",nature:"temporal",
  topic:"founder.execution.provenance"}]
Output (excerpt):
  "swot": { "weaknesses": [
    { "text": "An unresolved provenance question: the stated retail-analytics work history is not
      consistent with the public record for the pre-2021 period.", "claim_ids": ["f9"], "kind": "fact" } ] ... }
Why: the weakness cites the contradicted claim and is framed neutrally.

Undesired Example 1: uncited generic strength
Output (excerpt):
  "swot": { "strengths": [ { "text": "Strong, experienced founding team in a huge market.",
    "claim_ids": [], "kind": "fact" } ] ... }
Why this is wrong: "strong team", "huge market" cite nothing — generic, uncitable, padding. Rejected.
</examples>
</prompt>";
const SCHEMA_NAME = "memo_analytical";
const SCHEMA = {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["hypotheses", "swot"], "properties": {"hypotheses": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["statements"], "properties": {"statements": {"type": "array", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/statement"}}}}, "swot": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["strengths", "weaknesses", "opportunities", "threats"], "properties": {"strengths": {"type": "array", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/statement"}}, "weaknesses": {"type": "array", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/statement"}}, "opportunities": {"type": "array", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/statement"}}, "threats": {"type": "array", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/statement"}}}}}, "$defs": {"statement": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["text", "claim_ids", "kind"], "properties": {"text": {"type": "string"}, "claim_ids": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}, "kind": {"type": "string", "enum": ["fact", "not_disclosed", "benchmark", "structural"]}}}}};
const MODEL_NAME = "gpt-5.6-luna";

const inp = $input.first().json;
const userPayload = inp.pack_slice_analytical;

let parsed;
try {
  const r = await this.helpers.httpRequest({
    method: 'POST', url: 'https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions',
    headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + $env.OPENAI_API_KEY, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: {
      // gpt-5.6-luna rejects an explicit temperature (HTTP 400, verified live building
      // 03/04/05/08) -- omitted entirely, same convention every f0*-workflow LLM node in
      // this repo already follows.
      model: MODEL_NAME,
      messages: [ { role: 'system', content: SYS }, { role: 'user', content: JSON.stringify(userPayload) } ],
      response_format: { type: 'json_schema', json_schema: { name: SCHEMA_NAME, strict: true, schema: SCHEMA } },
    },
    json: true,
  });
  parsed = JSON.parse(r.choices[0].message.content);
} catch (e) {
  // Graceful degradation -- an LLM-node failure (network/schema/parse) must never
  // hard-fail the whole memo. This mirrors exactly the posture spec-review should-fix #1
  // put into lib/f06/assemble.js: this agent's keys are simply ABSENT from this item, and
  // [D]'s mergeSectionsParts()/backfillRequiredSections() supply the honest structural
  // fallback for any required section this agent would have written.
  parsed = {};
}
return [{ json: parsed }];

const SYS = "<prompt>
<title>
# PRE-SEED INVESTMENT-MEMO OPTIONAL SECTION WRITER (RISK · COMPETITION · FINANCIALS-LITE)
</title>

<description>
YOU WRITE THE OPTIONAL, EVIDENCE-CONDITIONAL SECTIONS OF A $100K PRE-SEED MEMO: A RISK
MATRIX, A COMPETITION SECTION, AND A FINANCIALS-LITE SECTION. YOU ONLY WRITE A SECTION
WHEN THE PACK GIVES YOU REAL MATERIAL FOR IT. FOR FINANCIALS YOU NEVER INVENT NUMBERS —
YOU STATE "NOT DISCLOSED" AND OFFER BENCHMARK COMPARABLES AS A LABELLED RANGE, NEVER A
VALUATION. WHEN A SECTION HAS NO INPUT, YOU OMIT IT.
</description>

<instructions>
## INSTRUCTIONS
1. INPUT VALIDATION (always first). Read the pack. Determine which optional sections have input:
   - `risk_matrix` has input if `gaps.contradictions` is non-empty OR there is >=1 material gap.
   - `competition` has input if there is >=1 claim with topic starting `competition.`.
   - `financials_lite` has input if there is a not_disclosed financial topic OR a usable benchmark
     is warranted (there almost always is — it is the honesty block).
   Set each section to `null` when it has no input, and `_sentinel: true` only when all three are
   null. ALL FOUR KEYS ARE ALWAYS EMITTED (strict mode) — a section is null, never omitted. Do not
   invent content.
2. `risk_matrix`: an array of risks. Each risk = {text, severity, likelihood, claim_ids}. Severity
   ∈ minor|moderate|material, likelihood ∈ low|medium|high. Each risk traces to a contradiction or a
   gap claim. Neutral framing.
3. `competition`: `statements` + `competitors[]`. For each `competition.competitor` claim, one entry
   {name, named_by_founder, claim_ids} — `named_by_founder` is the claim's `value.company_mentioned`
   (true = the founder named them; false = we surfaced them). A competitor the founder did NOT name
   is the highest-value item; include it. Use `competition.status_quo_alternative` claims as
   statements ("the real alternative today is a spreadsheet"). If no competition claims: set
   `competition` to `null` (never an empty competitors array).
   IMPORTANT: if you surface competitors, add one statement clarifying the list is what the evidence
   found, not necessarily exhaustive.
4. `financials_lite`: statements only.
   - Always include one not_disclosed statement for each absent financial topic (e.g.
     "Cap table: not disclosed.", "Revenue: not disclosed.").
   - Benchmark comparables (kind:"benchmark") ONLY as a labelled RANGE with the words
     "range, not a valuation" and a survivorship caveat. Never a point number, never tied to THIS
     company as if it were its real number.
5. Set any section with no input to `null` (all four keys always present). Emit JSON per schema.
</instructions>

<output_format>
## OUTPUT FORMAT
Return valid JSON only, matching memo-optional-json-schema.json. ALL FOUR KEYS ARE ALWAYS PRESENT
(strict mode). A section WITH input is an object; a section WITHOUT input is `null` (the assembler
drops null keys). `_sentinel` is `true` only when all three optional sections are null.
{
  "_sentinel": false,
  "risk_matrix":     { "risks": [ { "text":"...", "severity":"material", "likelihood":"high", "claim_ids":["<uuid>"] } ] },
  "competition":     { "statements": [ ... ], "competitors": [ { "name":"...", "named_by_founder": false, "claim_ids":["<uuid>"] } ] },
  "financials_lite": { "statements": [ { "text":"Cap table: not disclosed.", "claim_ids":[], "kind":"not_disclosed" } ] }
}
Example with no optional input at all:
{ "_sentinel": true, "risk_matrix": null, "competition": null, "financials_lite": null }
</output_format>

<chain_of_thoughts>
## CHAIN OF THOUGHTS
1. Read the pack. Check the three input conditions. If none hold, return {"_sentinel": true} and stop.
2. Risk matrix: turn each contradiction into a risk (severity = the contradiction's severity), and
   each material gap into a risk. Assign a plain likelihood. Cite the claim.
3. Competition: map each competition.competitor claim to a competitor entry, flag named_by_founder,
   put status_quo_alternative content into statements, add the non-exhaustive caveat.
4. Financials-lite: one not_disclosed line per absent money topic; then, if useful, one benchmark
   range statement with the mandatory caveat wording.
5. Drop uncitable facts; drop padding; ensure benchmark statements carry the caveat. Emit JSON.
</chain_of_thoughts>

<restrictions>
## WHAT NOT TO DO
- NEVER cite a claim_id outside `allowed_claim_ids`.
- NEVER invent a financial figure, valuation, or cap-table entry. Absent → not_disclosed.
- NEVER write a benchmark as a point number or without the "range, not a valuation" caveat.
- NEVER emit an empty section shell — set the key to `null` instead. If nothing at all: `_sentinel:true` with all three sections null.
- NEVER imply a surfaced competitor list is exhaustive; NEVER frame a not-named competitor as a "gotcha".
- NEVER output proceed / proceed-with-conditions / pass / watchlist / invest or any verdict.
- NEVER put a company-specific figure inside a not_disclosed/structural statement (benchmark ranges excepted, with caveat).
</restrictions>

<examples>
## FEW-SHOT EXAMPLES

Desired Example 1: financials honesty + benchmark range
Output (excerpt):
  "financials_lite": { "statements": [
    { "text": "Cap table: not disclosed.", "claim_ids": [], "kind": "not_disclosed" },
    { "text": "Revenue and burn: not disclosed.", "claim_ids": [], "kind": "not_disclosed" },
    { "text": "For context only: comparable pre-seed AI-infra rounds in 2024-2025 closed in a
      ~$6-12M post-money range (range, not a valuation; survivorship-biased toward funded outcomes).",
      "claim_ids": [], "kind": "benchmark" } ] }
Why: absences stated plainly; the comparable is an explicitly caveated range, never THIS company's number.

Desired Example 2: competitor the founder didn't name
Input (excerpt): claims=[{claim_id:"c3",topic:"competition.competitor",
  value:{company_mentioned:false,name:"Braintrust"},derived_status:"discovered"}]
Output (excerpt):
  "competition": { "statements": [ { "text": "Competitors below are what the evidence surfaced and
    may not be exhaustive.", "claim_ids": [], "kind": "structural" } ],
    "competitors": [ { "name": "Braintrust", "named_by_founder": false, "claim_ids": ["c3"] } ] }
Why: surfaces a non-founder-named competitor (high value), with a non-exhaustive caveat, neutrally.

Undesired Example 1: empty shell
Input (excerpt): no competition claims.
Output (excerpt): "competition": { "statements": [], "competitors": [] }
Why this is wrong: an empty section must be `null` (e.g. "competition": null), never an empty shell.
</examples>
</prompt>";
const SCHEMA_NAME = "memo_optional";
const SCHEMA = {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["_sentinel", "risk_matrix", "competition", "financials_lite"], "properties": {"_sentinel": {"type": "boolean"}, "risk_matrix": {"type": ["object", "null"], "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["risks"], "properties": {"risks": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["text", "severity", "likelihood", "claim_ids"], "properties": {"text": {"type": "string"}, "severity": {"type": "string", "enum": ["minor", "moderate", "material"]}, "likelihood": {"type": "string", "enum": ["low", "medium", "high"]}, "claim_ids": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}}}}}}, "competition": {"type": ["object", "null"], "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["statements", "competitors"], "properties": {"statements": {"type": "array", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/statement"}}, "competitors": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["name", "named_by_founder", "claim_ids"], "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"}, "named_by_founder": {"type": "boolean"}, "claim_ids": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}}}}}}, "financials_lite": {"type": ["object", "null"], "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["statements"], "properties": {"statements": {"type": "array", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/statement"}}}}}, "$defs": {"statement": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["text", "claim_ids", "kind"], "properties": {"text": {"type": "string"}, "claim_ids": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}, "kind": {"type": "string", "enum": ["fact", "not_disclosed", "benchmark", "structural"]}}}}};
const MODEL_NAME = "gpt-5.6-luna";

const inp = $input.first().json;
const userPayload = inp.pack_slice_optional;

let parsed;
try {
  const r = await this.helpers.httpRequest({
    method: 'POST', url: 'https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions',
    headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + $env.OPENAI_API_KEY, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: {
      // gpt-5.6-luna rejects an explicit temperature (HTTP 400, verified live building
      // 03/04/05/08) -- omitted entirely, same convention every f0*-workflow LLM node in
      // this repo already follows.
      model: MODEL_NAME,
      messages: [ { role: 'system', content: SYS }, { role: 'user', content: JSON.stringify(userPayload) } ],
      response_format: { type: 'json_schema', json_schema: { name: SCHEMA_NAME, strict: true, schema: SCHEMA } },
    },
    json: true,
  });
  parsed = JSON.parse(r.choices[0].message.content);
} catch (e) {
  // Graceful degradation -- an LLM-node failure (network/schema/parse) must never
  // hard-fail the whole memo. This mirrors exactly the posture spec-review should-fix #1
  // put into lib/f06/assemble.js: this agent's keys are simply ABSENT from this item, and
  // [D]'s mergeSectionsParts()/backfillRequiredSections() supply the honest structural
  // fallback for any required section this agent would have written.
  parsed = { _sentinel: true, risk_matrix: null, competition: null, financials_lite: null };
}
return [{ json: parsed }];

nodes.name: Decision
nodes.notes: SOURCE OF TRUTH: lib/f06/decision.js -- do not edit here, edit there and re-paste (via n8n/build-f06-workflow.py). No LLM call in this node.

nodes.name: Assemble + write
nodes.notes: SOURCE OF TRUTH: lib/f06/assemble.js -- do not edit here, edit there and re-paste (via n8n/build-f06-workflow.py).

parameters.content:
### PACK PROPAGATION (design.md SS5.1)
An n8n node emits what it RETURNS, not its input. [C]/[D] need
[A]'s pack numbers/ids -- reached via the $('Context pack') node
reference (valid: [A] is an ancestor of both via
[A]->...->Merge->[C]->[D]). [D] likewise reads the four section
groups with $('Merge').all(), merged by KEY/CONTENT inside
mergeSectionsParts(), never by input index.

### ALL FOUR [B] NODES ALWAYS EXECUTE (design.md SS5.3)
[B3] (memo-optional) is conditional in CONTENT, not in
EXECUTION -- with no qualifying input it emits the sentinel
{_sentinel:true, risk_matrix:null, competition:null,
financials_lite:null}, so the numberInputs:4 append-Merge
always sees all four inputs and never stalls (real
n8n-nodes-base.merge, branch i -> input i -- a plain multi-wire
fan-in silently runs only 1-2 branches in this n8n build,
TRACKER's own f03 finding).

nodes.name: Note: all four B nodes always execute

parameters.content:
### REQUIRED-SECTION BACK-FILL, NEVER REJECT (spec-review
should-fix #1, design.md SS9.3)
lib/f06/assemble.js's checkRequiredSections() was replaced with
backfillRequiredSections(): a missing/empty required section (or
SWOT array) gets ONE deterministic structural line instead of
rejecting the memo. As of task T6b, the citation gate and the
typed-exception guard ALSO no longer return {error} -- they
DROP + LOG the offending statement/item instead (dropped_count/
dropped_statements ride the memo_generated event payload), and
back-fill (this step) runs AFTER both drops so an emptied
required section still ships one line. assembleMemo()'s only
remaining {error} path is malformed input (pack/decision missing
entirely) -- an LLM omission or citation slip can never hard-fail
the whole memo any more.

### STRICT-MODE SCHEMAS (agents/README.md)
response_format uses strict:true for every [B] node -- every
agent JSON schema is run through this generator's own
strictify() at embed time (strips oneOf/allOf/min*/max*/
pattern/format, forces every property into `required`).
deep-dive-questions has NO *-json-schema.json file in this repo
-- its schema is reconstructed here from the prompt's own
OUTPUT FORMAT block + design.md SS4.3 (see QUESTIONS_SCHEMA_RAW
above), flagged as a design-ambiguity resolution.
